THIS WEEK IN LOCAL GOVERNMENT

Kronenwetter kicked off a new parks committee with plans for a major playground overhaul, while Wausau school officials and the teachers union opened contract negotiations for the coming school year.

  • Kronenwetter's new Parks Committee held its first meeting June 29, electing Erin Mishka chair and targeting a $150,000–$200,000 playground replacement at Bushka Park.
  • The committee scheduled a public parks tour for July 8 at 4 p.m. at the municipal center, with July 9 as a rain date.
  • A July 17 movie-under-the-stars fundraiser is planned to help support the Bushka Park playground project.
  • The Wausau Board of Education and the Wausau Education Association were set to exchange initial contract proposals for the 2026-27 school year at a July 1 public meeting.
  • Kronenwetter's CLIPP Committee is set to vote July 6 on revisions to its nuisance ordinance and consider traffic control changes on County Road XX and Terrebonne Drive.

Coming up: Monday brings a full slate, including Weston's Village Board and Community Life & Public Safety Committee and Kronenwetter's CLIPP meeting at 6 p.m. Later in the week, Marathon County's Infrastructure Committee meets Thursday and the City of Wausau's Infrastructure & Facilities and Sustainability committees also convene that evening.

Full summaries on the Meeting Tracker →
